findings.observation: PID 5678 (powershell.exe) appears in psscan output but is absent from pslist active process listing.
findings.interpretation: Possible hidden or terminated process artifact — requires corroboration with malfind and network analysis before concluding malicious activity.

tool_provenance.command: vol.py -f mem.raw windows.psscan; vol.py -f mem.raw windows.pslist; python3 graveyard_correlate.py --exports ./exports/

findings.observation: PID 1234 (cmd.exe) has an ESTABLISHED TCP connection to 203.0.113.5 on port 443.
findings.interpretation: External connection on HTTPS port may indicate C2 or legitimate web traffic — requires corroboration with threat intel and disk artifacts.

findings.observation: PID 5678 has an ESTABLISHED TCP connection to 198.51.100.42 on port 4444 but PID 5678 is absent from pslist.
findings.interpretation: Orphan socket — network connection without active process listing; may indicate process hollowing or recently terminated ghost process.
